question: ¿Qué STAB le da a Garchomp usar Earthquake?
ground_truth: Garchomp es tipo Tierra/Dragón; Earthquake es tipo Tierra, así que recibe STAB ×1.5 (o ×2.0 solo con Adaptability, que Garchomp no tiene por defecto).
contexts: STAB (Same-Type Attack Bonus) multiplica el daño del movimiento por 1.5 si el tipo del movimiento coincide con uno de los tipos del atacante.
Garchomp es Pokémon de tipos Dragón y Tierra. Earthquake es un movimiento de tipo Tierra.
Sin habilidades especiales, el multiplicador STAB estándar en generaciones recientes es 1.5×.

question: How much damage does max-roll Garchomp Earthquake do to standard defensive Blissey?
ground_truth: With random_factor 1.0 (max roll), the damage is 382, with a possible range of 325–382 HP.
contexts: Damage uses the Gen IX formula: level, stats, base power, modifiers (STAB, type effectiveness, weather, etc.), and a random factor between 0.85 and 1.0.
Garchomp commonly runs physical investment; Blissey often maximizes HP and special defense.
Ground is neutral against Normal-type defenders; Garchomp gains STAB on Earthquake.

question: ¿Por qué Ghost-type moves don't affect Normal types en combate?
ground_truth: Los movimientos tipo Fantasma son ineficaces (inmunidad) contra Pokémon tipo Normal: el daño efectivo es 0 salvo habilidades que ignoren inmunidades.
contexts: Type chart: Ghost deals 0× damage to Normal in standard Pokémon rules.
Normal in turn hits Ghost for 0× unless Foresight, Odor Sleuth, or Scrappy-style effects apply.
Immunities are applied before other modifiers in damage calculation.

question: Damage: Garchomp Ice Fang vs offensively invested Salamence, max roll?
ground_truth: Ice Fang is super-effective on Dragon/Flying; expect a high physical roll. Max-roll damage is 408 (range 346–408).
contexts: Ice hits Flying for super-effective (2×) and Dragon for super-effective (2×); combined on Dragon/Flying yields 4× before other modifiers.
Ice Fang may flinch but damage calc focuses on power and modifiers first.
Salamence keeps weaker physical bulk than many walls.

question: What typing is Charizard and how does Fire interact with Grass/Poison Venusaur?
ground_truth: Charizard is Fire/Flying. Fire moves are super-effective against Grass (2×) and neutral against Poison (1×), so overall about 2× against Grass/Poison.
contexts: Dual typings multiply matchup effectiveness: each type factor applies.
Fire resists Fire, Grass, Ice, Fairy; weak to Water, Electric, Rock.
Venusaur is Grass/Poison and often used as a special tank.

question: Timid Charizard Flamethrower vs Modest Venusaur, clear weather, max roll?
ground_truth: Special Fire vs Grass/Poison with STAB: max damage 309 (range 262–309).
contexts: Flamethrower has 90 base power and is special.
No Sun in this baseline: Fire moves use standard modifiers.
STAB applies when move type matches attacker typing (Fire/Flying uses Fire STAB on Flamethrower).

question: Same matchup Charizard Flamethrower vs Venusaur but under harsh sunlight?
ground_truth: Sun boosts Fire-type moves; max-roll damage becomes 463 (range 393–463).
contexts: Harsh sunlight multiplies Fire-type attacks in modern formulas unless cancelled by other effects.
Water moves are weakened in sun; Fire moves strengthen.
Venusaur may run Thick Fat in some formats but not in our canonical fixture.

question: ¿Qué región introduce a Oshawott como inicial de agua?
ground_truth: Oshawott es el inicial Agua de la quinta generación, en la región Teselia (Unova).
contexts: Generation V starters: Snivy, Tepig, Oshawott.
Unova is inspired by New York metropolitan aesthetics in early route design.
Professor Juniper distributes starter Pokémon in Black and White.

question: Modest Venusaur Hydro Pump vs Timid Charizard in rain, max roll?
ground_truth: Rain powers Water moves; Hydro Pump reaches 447 damage max (379–447).
contexts: Hydro Pump is special with 110 base power.
Rain traditionally boosts Water-type attack damage while weakening Fire.
Charizard is weak to Rock and Electric; Water hits Fire super-effectively.

question: Venusaur Hydro Pump vs Charizard with no weather, max roll?
ground_truth: Without rain, Hydro Pump still hits Fire/Flying super-effectively: 298 max (253–298).
contexts: Water vs Fire is 2×; Water vs Flying is neutral; combined yields 2× on Fire/Flying.
Investment on Venusaur favors special attack in the canonical fixture.
Field conditions default to clear in the baseline battle.

question: Timid Gengar Thunderbolt vs defensive Blissey, max roll?
ground_truth: Electric is neutral vs Normal; expect 82 damage at max roll (69–82).
contexts: Thunderbolt is special, 90 BP.
Blissey has enormous special bulk and HP.
Ghost/Poison Gengar uses special moves well with high SpA.

question: Porygon-Z with Adaptability using Hyper Voice vs Blissey, max roll?
ground_truth: Adaptability raises STAB from 1.5 to 2.0 on Normal moves; Hyper Voice yields 184 max (156–184).
contexts: Adaptability doubles STAB multiplier relative to standard for matching types.
Hyper Voice is a 90 BP special Normal move in our canonical fixture.
Porygon-Z has high special attack but frail defenses.

question: Name one common OU role for Ferrothorn and why entry hazards matter.
ground_truth: Ferrothorn often acts as a defensive hazard setter (Stealth Rock, Spikes) because of strong physical bulk and Iron Barbs chip; hazards punish switches.
contexts: OU (OverUsed) is a Smogon tier balancing strong but non-legendary staples.
Hazards deal damage or chip when opponents switch, shaping long games.
Grass/Steel typing gives key resistances but a Fire weakness.

question: ¿Cuánto daño hace Garchomp con Earthquake contra Blissey defensiva estándar? (máximo del rango)
ground_truth: Con random_factor 1.0, el máximo es 382 HP de daño (rango 325–382).
contexts: Los informes del calculator_agent incluyen rango y porcentajes de HP del defensor.
La fórmula Gen IX usa factor aleatorio 0.85–1.0 salvo que se fije.
Garchomp tiene alto Ataque base; Earthquake tiene 100 de poder.

question: Explain briefly what EVs cap at per stat in modern VGC.
ground_truth: A Pokémon can distribute up to 510 EVs total with a maximum of 252 EVs in any one stat in Gen IX-style rules used competitively.
contexts: Effort Values modify final stats at level 100.
Competitive builders optimize 252/252/4 spreads often.
VGC is doubles format with item and move restrictions per regulation.

question: Who is the protagonist's rival in Pokémon Ruby/Sapphire near the start?
ground_truth: The main rival is Brendan if you play May, or May if you play Brendan—both appear as neighbor rivals in Hoenn.
contexts: Hoenn features Professor Birch and two playable protagonists.
Rival teams mirror starter typings for early battles.
The story involves Team Aqua and Team Magma environmental conflicts.

question: What item doubles Speed on the first turn of battle for many sweepers?
ground_truth: Choice Scarf multiplies Speed by 1.5× while locking the holder into one move until switched.
contexts: Choice items trade flexibility for a large stat boost.
Scarf users often pivot with U-turn or Volt Switch.
Competitive builds pair scarf with coverage moves carefully.

question: ¿Qué tipo es efectivo contra Agua + Volador a la vez?
ground_truth: El tipo Eléctrico suele ser 4× contra Agua/Volador porque es supereficaz contra ambos tipos (2× × 2×).
contexts: Dual-type matchups multiply individual type factors.
Water/Flying is common on Pokémon like Gyarados before Mega evolution changes.
Rock moves can also be threatening due to Flying weakness.

question: Damage check: ice fang salamence from chomp again for regression?
ground_truth: 408 max damage; range 346–408.
contexts: Regression tests repeat canonical battles to catch formula drift.
Ice coverage is standard on many Dragon attackers.
Salamence's Intimidate is ignored in raw damage fixtures unless modeled.

question: What Ability halves Fire and Ice damage in some Generations?
ground_truth: Thick Fat halves damage from Fire-type and Ice-type moves.
contexts: Abilities can modify type effectiveness indirectly via damage multipliers.
Thick Fat appears on Pokémon like Miltank and some Venusaur forms depending on game.
Our Venusaur canonical build uses standard ability fixtures elsewhere.

question: Why might you run Rapid Spin or Defog in OU?
ground_truth: Both remove hazards (Spikes, Stealth Rock layers, etc.) or lower evasion in Defog's case—keeping your switches healthier.
contexts: Entry hazard stacking punishes teams without removal.
OU balances offensive pressure with defensive pivoting.
Some Pokémon get both but choose one slot due to moveset limits.

question: ¿Cuál es el daño máximo de Flamethrower de Charizard a Venusaur bajo sol fuerte?
ground_truth: 463 HP máximo; rango aproximado 393–463.
contexts: El clima modifica multiplicadores de tipo de movimiento activados por habilidades o movimientos como Sunny Day.
Charizard es especial ofensivo en el fixture canónico.
Venusaur puede beneficiarse del sol con Chlorophyll en otros sets.

question: Who is the Champion in Paldea's Pokémon Scarlet/Violet base story?
ground_truth: Geeta holds the title of Champion in Paldea for much of the campaign; the player eventually challenges the Elite Four and Champion circuit.
contexts: Paldea is inspired by Iberia with open-world progression.
Victory Road culminates in League assessment tests in Gen IX.
Area Zero ties late-game lore about paradox Pokémon.

question: Mixed question: state Garchomp's key attacking stat and one common STAB physical move.
ground_truth: Garchomp leans on high Attack (base 130) and often runs Earthquake or Dragon-type physical coverage for STAB.
contexts: Pseudo-legendary dragons offer strong speed and offense.
Rough Skin or Sand Veil historically define competitive sets before ability bans.
Items like Life Orb or Choice Band amplify damage output.

question: How does Terastallizing change damage typing at snapshot time?
ground_truth: Terastallizing lets a Pokémon change its offensive Tera Type for STAB calculations on moves while keeping defensive typing until the swap resolves per battle rules (simplified; exact rules vary by format).
contexts: Gen IX introduced Tera types separate from base typings.
Competitive VGC regulations rotate allowed Tera users.
STAB bonuses can change when Terastallization matches move type.

question: Benchmark: Gen Timid Gengar Thunderbolt vs Blissey wall.
ground_truth: 82 damage at max random roll; range 69–82.
contexts: Special walls like Blissey define tiering benchmarks.
Thunderbolt coverage hits Water/Flying common threats.
Choice Specs Gengar would exceed these numbers.

question: What is the type of the move Shadow Ball?
ground_truth: Shadow Ball is a Ghost-type special move.
contexts: Ghost coverage hits Psychic and Ghost types super-effectively.
Special attacking Ghosts like Gengar use it frequently.
Some formats ban King's Rock interactions with multi-hit moves separately.

question: Why is Speed tie order important in doubles?
ground_truth: Turn order decides Who moves first when stats tie after modifiers—a coin flip or PRNG tie can swing Protect reads or knockouts.
contexts: Doubles amplifies synergy between partners.
Trick Room inverts speed ordering entirely.
Tailwind temporarily doubles team Speed for several turns.

question: Rain-boosted Hydro from Venusaur to Charizard — recheck numbers?
ground_truth: 447 on max roll with rain (379–447).
contexts: Weather setters like Pelipper enable rain cores.
Grass/ Poison typings resist common powder moves.
Charizard's 4× Rock weakness is separate from this calculation.

question: Closing sanity: list the five orchestrator intents this project uses.
ground_truth: stats, calc, lore, strategy, mixed.
contexts: The orchestrator classifies queries before dispatching specialist agents.
Calculator intent routes to deterministic damage math without LLM guessing.
Verifier and synthesizer nodes normalize outputs for the API/UI.
